council_result: S125 Full 3-way Council R1+R2 (GROK-DISRUPTOR grok-4.20-0309-reasoning + GEMINI-AUDITOR gemini-3.1-pro-preview) + PRIME-AUDIT + CHECK. R1: transport disconnect->reconnect race (Grok CRIT/Gemini HIGH) REFUTED by S82 shipped precedent; ClientSync-reuse contamination (Gemini MED) FALSE PREMISE; 'permanent demotion' cascade (Grok HIGH) REFUTED (seat 0 warrant-excluded, follows as client). ADOPTED: unify demoteToClient({reestablishTransport}), explicit clientSync=null hardening, existing overlay state-machine (no terminal latch), rate 90/40 + intentThrottled type-log, cascade+burst tests. R2: all HOLD, zero residual. CHECK (2-way, Gemini timed out): Grok HIGH (currentEpoch ordering) REFUTED (conflates session.currentEpoch vs ClientSync private currentEpoch; connectAsClient->fenceRejoinEpoch synchronous), Grok MED (limiter stale tokens) NON-ISSUE (client never consults limiter; per-page not cross-peer). 15th #triage-external-criticals-against-exact-arithmetic.

Council Full R1+R2: transport-race (Grok CRIT) refuted by S82 precedent + inert self-gating old host handlers; 'permanent demotion' (Grok HIGH) refuted (seat 0 warrant-excluded via beginMatch 'if e.seat===0 continue', computeClaimDelayMs->null, follows as client via stored warrant); Gemini ClientSync-reuse FALSE PREMISE (original host never had a ClientSync). Reuse-first: demoteToClient unified with reestablishTransport; the rejoin IS the shipped S82 auto-reconnect path (already e2e-proven), so it inherits proven robustness. LOCKED §13.21 v1->v2, HOST_MIGRATION_DESIGN §12 as-built + §10 Q1 resolved.

reflexion_entries_to_archive.lesson: Both Council seats filed high-severity criticals on the zombie-rejoin (transport-tear race, permanent demotion, buffer contamination); every one dissolved once the design was recognized as a REUSE of the already-shipped, already-e2e-proven S82 auto-reconnect path rather than new machinery. The deposed original host is just a seat-0 (warrant-excluded) peer that re-runs connectAsClient — so it inherits reconnect's proven robustness and its correctness reduces to two existing, tested invariants (fresh-ClientSync epoch fence + seat-0 can't-ladder). The freeze-thaw e2e passed first run because the mechanism was 90% shipped code. When a 'new' feature can be expressed as re-pointing an existing wired path, the review surface collapses to the seam, not the whole subsystem.

Gemini's priority-inversion finding (a burst could drop a real placement while spending tokens on idempotent avatar-pos) was legitimate, but the proportionate fix for a Med/Small hardening item was to SIZE a single bucket above the measured worst legit burst (avatar-pos is 10Hz SENDER-throttled -> a 3s unfreeze buffers ~38 intents « capacity 90) rather than build a two-bucket QoS subsystem. Measuring the legit ceiling first turned a 'needs QoS architecture' finding into a one-constant decision + a documented v2 escalation lever. Reach for structure only after the number says the simple guard can't hold.
